_comment: Worked examples embedded in the Semantic Mode component-proposal prompt (A4). Per v2 plan addendum, removing the few-shot regressed AI output to flat 3-node graphs; the worked example below shows the LLM what a rich 6-component decomposition looks like, with a mix of all five supported distribution families (beta, normal, uniform, lognormal, triangular) and explicit dependsOn relationships. The component-proposal step deliberately omits mean/SD/range — those come from Phase B research. The example is static reference data, not product data; it is inlined verbatim into the system prompt by lib/semantic/propose-components.ts and never sent through any code path that consumes it as live LLM output.

examples.userQuery: Will our Q3 product launch hit 10k signups in the first month?

clarifications.question: What product segment is this? B2B SaaS, consumer mobile, hardware, or something else?
clarifications.answer: B2B SaaS for fleet logistics operators.

clarifications.question: Are you counting signups including waitlist conversions, or only post-launch net-new users?
clarifications.answer: Tracking only post-launch new signups (not waitlist).

clarifications.question: What is your current pre-launch funnel volume (website visits, waitlist size, pre-orders)?
clarifications.answer: Current pre-launch funnel: 2k website visits/month, 400 waitlist signups.

components.name: Baseline website traffic
components.description: Monthly visits to the marketing site at launch time.
components.why: All conversion percentages multiply against this — a 30% miss here cascades through everything.

components.name: Launch traffic lift
components.description: Multiplier on baseline traffic from launch announcements and PR.
components.why: Most likely +2x; worst case flat; best case +5x. Bounded by realistic PR reach.

components.name: Waitlist conversion rate
components.description: Share of pre-launch waitlist members who actually sign up.
components.why: Bounded in [0,1] and historically variable (10-60%) across B2B SaaS.

components.name: Cold visitor signup rate
components.description: Share of net-new visitors who sign up after one visit.
components.why: Drives the bulk of the count for product launches with broad reach.

components.name: Competitor counter-launch probability
components.description: Probability a major competitor announces a comparable product in the same window.
components.why: If it fires, conversion rates compress 20-40%.

components.name: Conversion drag if competitor responds
components.description: Multiplicative drag on cold_visitor_conversion when the competitor fires.
